message:
refactor(buffer): replace borrow-based polling with mem::replace state machine

Rewrite BufferFuture::poll to use std::mem::replace for state
transitions instead of borrowing through &mut this.state. The
previous approach had borrow-conflict issues when the
WaitingForReady branch needed to take the request while also
borrowing shared, leading to a split-out is_waiting check with
an unreachable!() fallback.

Now each loop iteration takes ownership of the current state via
mem::replace (temporarily setting Done), then matches on the
owned value and sets the new state explicitly. This eliminates
all borrow conflicts and the unreachable!() code path.

Also updates test type parameters from &str error types to
std::convert::Infallible + EchoService to match the new
S: Service<R> bound on BufferFuture.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
